name: Mintywiches
recipeIngredient: Andes mint wafers; broken in half
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine butter, granulated sugar, and egg in a large mixing bowl and beat until light and creamy.
2. Blend in melted chocolate chips and corn syrup.
3. Sift together flour, baking soda, and salt.
4. Stir the sifted dry ingredients into the chocolate mixture.
5. Chill the dough overnight. It will be quite firm.
6. Using a round scoop about 1/4 teaspoon, shape the dough into balls.
7. Roll each ball in granulated sugar.
8. Repeat with all of the dough and place the cookies on ungreased cookie sheets.
9. Bake at 325°F for 8-10 minutes.
10. Remove each batch from the oven and let stand just a few seconds.
11. Turn half of each batch upside down and top each cookie with a mint wafer half.
12. Top them with the second cookie, making a sandwich with two mint wafers and Andes candy.
13. Place the Mintywiches on racks to allow the filling to harden. Twist slightly so that you maintain the "roundness" of the mint.
Hint: The dough becomes sticky and difficult to work with at room temperature. Return any unused portions to the refrigerator between batches.
